transcript: Tonight law enforcement agencies across Southern California are warning of a group of criminals believed to be responsible for a string of burglaries in several communities including Thousand Oaks and Riverside. In a story you'll see only on 5, Rick Chambers spoke to some of the victims of those burglaries. He joins us live from Thousand Oaks tonight. Rick. Yeah Micah, this is a group it's thought anyway to be from South America that's been striking all around Southern California. We're in the East Hills neighborhood of Thousand Oaks tonight. This is a place that got hit about 7.30 yesterday evening. It's believed that these thieves may have come up off a local golf course. But police are telling everybody tonight expect an increase in this type of thing as the holidays approach. And it kind of struck I have three guys my backyard James Fitzpatrick describes the moment his security system notified him at work Wednesday evening that his Thousand Oaks home was being burglarized and he watched on his phone as three mass thieves started breaking in they boosted one of the guys up to the second floor window open here and i could hear them breaking the window when the burglars couldn't enter through that upstairs window they focused on the patio slider they just kicked out the bottom of the door they busted it out with something and you could just hear the glass just shattering footprints in the backyard indicate that the robbers probably climbed some 400 feet up from the sunset hills golf course to get to the house they had so much mud on their shoes that they stepped in the broken glass it was all over the house and these photos show the aftermath just about every room ransacked there wasn't a cabinet that wasn't just stripped everything was on the floors james son sean was at volleyball practice when the break-in went down We'll be right back. room ransacked there wasn't a cabinet that wasn't just stripped everything was on the floors James son Sean was at volleyball practice when the break-in went down the thieves only grabbing costume jewelry but also his CIF ring he said he's actually more surprised by what they didn't take my Mac was sitting on the counter and they just went by it he went to my dad's room his Apple watch sitting on the bedside table right by it. There was a little cash jar I keep in the closet. They went right by it. It was kind of surprising. Police responded so quickly after James' 911 call that the burglars were caught in the act. They were in the backyard before they came down the stairs from upstairs quick response yeah quite a response ventura county sheriff's deputies captured all three of the thieves thought to be part of a chilean gang and may be responsible for two similar break-ins in riverside last month all had burglary tools and fake travel documents on them at the time and are being held now on a 150 000 bail so three people arrested last night two grown men and a 14 year old boy Thank you for watching. time and are being held now on a $150,000 bail. So three people arrested last night, two grown men and a 14-year-old boy in Thousand Oaks. I'm Rick Chambers. Guys, let me throw it back to you in Hollywood. Rick, thank you.
